cards.back: A data structure in which each element points to the next element in the sequence.
Both linear (sequential) and circular linked lists are possible.
Insertion involves adding a new node at a given position in the sequence.
Traversing involves visiting all elements in the sequence, starting from the first element.
Delete operation involves removing a node from the sequence.
Linked list can support various operations such as insertion, deletion, and traversal.
A data structure in which each node contains a reference to the first node of the list.
Linked lists are used in various applications including database queries, compiler design, and web browsers.
